channel.title: Kano Agent Backlog Skill
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Kano Agent Backlog Skill

item.title: Releases
item.description: Releases Published release notes and change history Release 0.0.3 Release 0.0.1 Changelog .

item.title: changelog
item.description: Changelog All notable changes to kano-agent-backlog-skill will be documented in this file. This project uses Git tags as releases: vX.Y.Z. The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.
0.0.1 — Initial local-first backlog workflow (MVP) This is the first public cut of the Kano Agent Backlog demo workspace: a local-first, file-based backlog that supports agent collaboration ...

0.0.3 — Config/Profiles hardening + effective-config artifacts + release gate stabilization Release target: 0.0.3. Previous released version: 0.0.2. This release also aligns public and native-facing version metadata on the 0.0.3 line so the repository no longer presents 0.1.0 as the current public release.

item.description: API Reference Python API documentation API Overview Full API Documentation .

item.description: CLI Reference Command-line interface documentation CLI Commands .

item.title: Technical References
item.description: Technical References Technical documentation and references bases config-simplification context_graph embedding_pipeline gitignore indexing logging processes project-config-implementation ...

item.title: workflow
item.description: Workflow SOP A) Planning (discussion → tickets) Pre-planning: Sync DB sequences Before creating any work items, ensure the DB sequence is synchronized: # Sync sequences from filesystem ...

item.title: views
item.description: Views (Obsidian Dataview) If you want fewer plugin dependencies, consider Obsidian Bases as a plugin-free alternative for table-style dashboards. See references/bases.md.

item.title: templates
item.description: Templates Work item template Place the file under _kano/backlog/items/<type>/. Use the ID prefix derived from config/profile.env → PROJECT_NAME (example: kano-agent-backlog-skill-demo → KABSD).
